title: Versioned Migrations, Expand-Contract Changes, and Rollback Evidence · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Migration Ledger
summary: Developers edit production tables manually, migrations mix irreversible data loss with schema changes, deploys assume zero old binaries, and rollback has never run. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go migration ledger increment in rehearsed schema and data migration chain. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Keep ordered immutable migration files, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, and applied-state inspection under revision control.
Include tenant ownership in keys, constraints, queries, tests, and audit evidence so cross-tenant references cannot form.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Migration Ledger · Theory Feed Go Migration Ledger
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5957 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, bounded backfill, and recovery; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
